2011-06-27.abstract: The government threatened to bar from Israel any foreign journalist who boards a flotilla scheduled to depart this week that seeks to challenge an Israeli naval blockade of Gaza.

2011-06-27.title: In Zimbabwe, Mugabe Government Is Not Sharing the Power to Arrest Rivals
2011-06-27.abstract: A judge in Zimbabwe freed Jameson Timba, a member of the Movement for Democratic Change party who was arrested after being accused of calling President Robert Mugabe a liar.

2011-06-27.title: Spike in U.S. Deaths in Iraq Raises Worries
2011-06-27.abstract: The June total of combat-related deaths of American soldiers reportedly is now 11, the most since May 2009, when 12 were killed.

2011-06-27.title: Egyptian Leader Assures McCain and Kerry on Transition
2011-06-27.abstract: Senators John McCain and John Kerry said that Egypt’s interim military rulers wanted to transfer powers to an elected government “as soon as possible.”

2011-06-28.title: Long-Serving Finance Minister Calls for Reforms to Bolster Russia’s Power
2011-06-28.abstract: Aleksei L. Kudrin speaks for an important group: well-placed Russian elites who are advocating for change from within the system.

2011-06-28.title: Elders Offer Help at Japan’s Crippled Reactor
2011-06-28.abstract: Older engineers say that their age puts them at less risk when exposed to radiation.

2011-06-28.title: Tanzania: No Serengeti Highway
2011-06-28.abstract: The Tanzanian government has indicated that it will not build a highway through the Serengeti National Park.

2011-06-28.title: As Yemen Teeters From Political Unrest, a Humanitarian Crisis May Not Be Far Off
2011-06-28.abstract: A breakdown in public services and a shortage of fuel have led to blackouts, rationing and a growing despair.

2011-06-28.title: Q. and A. with Aleksei L. Kudrin
2011-06-28.abstract: The third most powerful figure in the Kremlin speaks about politics and the economy.

2011-06-29.title: In Turkey, Lawmakers Refuse Oath in Protest
2011-06-29.abstract: Some 170 deputies refused to take their oaths of office in response to court rulings that barred eight of their colleagues, currently in jail on terrorism-related charges, from joining the assembly.

2011-06-29.title: Libyan Base Falls to a Rebel Raid in the West
2011-06-29.abstract: Rebels in the Nafusah Mountain region overtook a base in an raid that raised morale but provided little in captured weaponry.

2011-06-29.title: Greece Demonstrations
2011-06-29.abstract: Protesters hurled rocks and fire bombs at riot police in central Athens on Tuesday as a general strike against new austerity measures brought the country to a standstill.

2011-06-29.title: Egyptian Court Orders Dissolution of Mubarak-Era Local Councils
2011-06-29.abstract: The order was a major step toward dismantling one of the last political institutions installed by the former president, Hosni Mubarak.

2011-06-29.title: A Parliament on the Move Grows Costly
2011-06-29.abstract: Plenary sessions have members traveling to Strasbourg, France, every month despite growing cries of waste.

2011-06-30.title: Arrests in Moldova Over Possible Uranium Smuggling
2011-06-30.abstract: A six-member group had been negotiating the sale, police officials said, and the authorities suggested that the material had come from Russia.

2011-06-30.title: Strategic Site Is Captured by Militants in Yemen
2011-06-30.abstract: The insurgents took over a sports stadium that had been used to store food and other provisions for about 2,000 government soldiers.

2011-06-30.title: China: Tibetans Are Arrested After New Protests in Sichuan
2011-06-30.abstract: At least 30 Tibetans have been detained in 15 incidents of protest this month in a restive part of Sichuan Province, in western China.

2011-06-30.title: Senegal: Soldiers Sent Into Streets of Capital After Fiery Riots
2011-06-30.abstract: Senegal has deployed troops around ministry buildings and an important route in the capital, Dakar, a spokesman said Wednesday, in a bid to forestall further rioting.

2011-06-30.title: Notion of Independence Makes Halting Progress
2011-06-30.abstract: The Scottish National Party, whose avowed goal is securing Scottish independence, now controls Parliament, although a referendum could create a dilemma for the party’s leader.
